business_description.nature_of_business: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus.

coverages_discussed.notes: Coverage is provided through Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation per state statute.
coverages_discussed.requested_limit: Statutory limits with employer's liability of 1M/1M/1M

coverages_discussed.notes: Occurrence form including standard premises/operations and products/completed operations.
Business Auto covering all owned, hired, and non-owned vehicles.
Coverage for all scheduled locations on a special form with replacement cost valuation.
Following form over primary GL, Auto, and Employers Liability.
Coverage requested as per expiring program.

document_info.prepared_by: David Reinhardt, Vice President, Transportation Insurance Specialists

extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 48 total claims with 1378733.13 in total paid losses and 1503069.19 total incurred. Currently, 10 claims remain open and 38 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9%. Breakdown by coverage line: Workers' Compensation: 24 claims, 826622.56 paid, 888785.06 incurred; General Liability: 5 claims, 54126.51 paid, 54126.51 incurred; Commercial Auto: 16 claims, 387376.46 paid, 449560.02 incurred; Commercial Property: 2 claims, 106691.36 paid, 106691.36 incurred; Umbrella / Excess: 0 claims, 0.00 paid, 0.00 incurred; Motor Truck Cargo: 1 claim, 3916.24 paid, 3916.24 incurred.
extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Great Lakes Freight Systems, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, with approximately 38 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property, Umbrella / Excess, Motor Truck Cargo.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually; a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits; an employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative in place for over five years; contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements; a favorable third-party risk control survey completed within the past 12 months; a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%; security cameras, sprinkler and 24/7 monitoring services at all locations; a structured HR program with low turnover and consistent safety training; and a documented preventive maintenance schedule for its fleet.

concerns: Loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period.
Handwritten note on page 2 states: Need docs.
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: 48 total claims with 1378733.13 total paid losses and 1503069.19 total incurred; 10 claims remain open and 38 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio stated as 12.9%.
positives: Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually.
Dedicated safety committee meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits.
Employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative have been in place for over five years.
Contractual risk transfer provisions are included in all subcontractor and vendor agreements.
Third-party risk control survey completed within the past 12 months with favorable results.
Formal return-to-work program has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%.
All locations are equipped with security cameras, sprinkler and 24/7 monitoring services.
485 employees with structured HR program, low turnover, and consistent safety training.
Fleet of 37 vehicles is maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule.
